name: Geschwindigkeitsüberwachung Neigetechnik (GNT)
summary: GNT supervises higher permitted speeds for authorised tilting trains on suitably equipped routes. It supplements rather than replaces ordinary signalling and train protection.
playerRecognition: A GNT or Neigetechnik indication is active in the cab.
The permitted speed may exceed the conventional speed shown for non-tilting trains.
The train may display GNT-specific supervision or fault indications depending on the cab.
playerActions: Enable GNT only when the vehicle, service and route support it.
Follow the train's supervised permitted speed and all lineside signals.
Do not assume a tilting train may always use the higher speed; GNT must be available and active.
If GNT becomes unavailable, obey the lower conventional speed profile and any train-specific transition instructions.

relationships.pzb: PZB can remain relevant for lineside restrictions and signal-related supervision.
relationships.lzb: On LZB sections, cab supervision may govern movement independently of GNT route speed benefits.
relationships.tilt: Physical body tilt and GNT speed supervision are related but not the same function. A visible tilt does not prove that higher-speed supervision is active.

tswNotes: Controls, annunciators and activation steps are specific to the simulated train.
The DB BR 411 ICE T on Frankfurt–Fulda is the initial TSW profile targeted by this project.
GNT relevance varies by route section and timetable service.
openQuestions: Which Frankfurt–Fulda sections expose active GNT supervision in TSW?
Which BR 411 cab indications and controls are fully functional in TSW 6?
How does the game handle loss or isolation of GNT during a service?
